The artifact has redundant novelty assertions ('first… first… first'), mixed register, and structural repetition that writing polish would resolve."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvement focus on surface writing quality (clarity, concision, flow, eliminating redundant 'first' claims), or do you also want deeper content review — verifying that the quantitative results are logically grounded in the described setup and that the novelty framing holds against plausible prior work on query routing?"}

rationale.R1.2: The draft builds a causal chain — ambiguity classifier → typed dispatch → latency and GPU-hour gains; improving the draft requires checking that intermediates and conclusions cohere (e.g., the leap from 7ms classifier overhead to 19% p95 improvement is asserted, not derived).
rationale.R1.3: Five specific quantitative claims (38% tail-round reduction, 19% p95 improvement, 7ms overhead, 22% GPU-hour reduction, 10M-query trace) appear without visible methodology anchors in this excerpt; aligning claims to evidence is central to any credible improvement pass.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most directly targets sentence and paragraph quality — the natural, load-bearing reading of the delegation. The artifact has redundant novelty assertions ('first… first… first'), mixed register, and structural repetition that writing polish would resolve.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement focus on surface writing quality (clarity, concision, flow, eliminating redundant 'first' claims), or do you also want deeper content review — verifying that the quantitative results are logically grounded in the described setup and that the novelty framing holds against plausible prior work on query routing?
